the_signal.headline: Multi-agent debugging becomes the GTM blocker nobody's solving
the_signal.body: n8n's Production AI Playbook surfaces what every revenue ops team is hitting: three agents work fine, four agents become a debugging nightmare. This is the operational ceiling breaking GTM teams right now—not model capability, but visibility into multi-agent failure modes. Without debuggability, you can't scale agent-driven revenue workflows beyond pilot stage.

tool_drop.name: cognee + n8n Self-Improving Agent Skills
tool_drop.what: Workflow framework that catches failed review runs, proposes skill fixes, and auto-updates instructions after approval.
tool_drop.why: This is how you operationalize agent iteration—critical for GTM teams running quality gates on outbound, lead routing, or deal scoring agents that drift in production.

thread_of_day.title: Enterprise AI Governance is now table-stakes for compliance
thread_of_day.takeaway: Organizations scaling AI into revenue workflows can't ship without governance tooling—audit trails, access controls, and model tracking are no longer nice-to-have for GTM ops.

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.
quote.source: n8n Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns

whats_moving.context: As teams move from single-agent pilots to production multi-agent stacks, observability and failure mode management become the hard problem blocking scale.
Teams are moving from static agent definitions to feedback loops that detect failure and auto-propose fixes—this is how GTM agents stay accurate over time.
Enterprise buying now requires compliance tooling baked into revenue workflows; teams without audit trails and access controls can't close deals with regulated buyers.

act_on_this: Map your current AI agents by failure mode—what breaks when? Then evaluate whether you have visibility into why. If you're running 3+ agents in production without centralized failure detection, you're one bad quarter away from a compliance audit disaster.

numbers.stat: 5-level AI maturity framework across 4 dimensions (Usage, Integration, Governance, ROI tracking)
numbers.context: Most GTM teams are between levels 1-2 (ad-hoc experimentation); scaling to level 3+ requires governance and orchestration infrastructure most haven't built yet.
